notes: World Cup goalscoring age-record trio (on-device request). Messi = per-country (youngest AND oldest for his country), Pelé = youngest overall, Roger Milla = oldest overall ($1000 deep answer).

Messi's per-country record is cited to ESPN ('Messi is now both the youngest and the oldest Argentinean player to score at a World Cup'); the youngest leg + 2026 goal are also on the Wikipedia goals list. The clue keeps 'his country's' but omits 'Argentina' to make it harder — Messi is NOT youngest/oldest overall, so the per-country qualifier is required for accuracy.

Pelé (youngest overall, vs Wales 1958, age 17; only 3-time World Cup winner) and Roger Milla (oldest overall, at 42 vs Russia in 1994; Italia '90 corner-flag dance) are grounded in their Wikipedia pages.

questions.clue: After scoring at the 2026 World Cup, this soccer star is now both his country's youngest and the oldest player ever to score a World Cup goal.

citations.title: World Cup 2026 overreactions and takeaways
citations.snippet: Messi is now both the youngest and the oldest Argentinean player to score at a World Cup.

citations.title: List of international goals scored by Lionel Messi
citations.snippet: His goal against Serbia and Montenegro, on 16 June 2006, at the age of 18 years and 358 days, made him the youngest-ever scorer for Argentina at a FIFA World Cup. ... 16 June 2026 — Algeria — 2026 FIFA World Cup.

questions.clue: In 1958, at just 17, this forward became the youngest player ever to score at a World Cup, netting against Wales — and he remains the only man to win three World Cups.

citations.title: Pelé
citations.snippet: he became the tournament's youngest goalscorer after scoring against Wales in the quarter-finals [1958]. ... he won three FIFA World Cup titles – in 1958, 1962, and 1970 – becoming the only player to do so.

questions.clue: At 42, this Cameroonian became the oldest man ever to score at a World Cup, striking against Russia in 1994 — four years after his hip-swiveling corner-flag dances made him the breakout star of Italia '90.

citations.title: Roger Milla
citations.snippet: at the age of 42, Milla broke his own record as the oldest goalscorer in World Cup by scoring against Russia in the 1994 FIFA World Cup. ... Milla frequently celebrated goals by running to the corner flag and performing a dance similar to the lambada.
